steps.title: Identify the signal about trust
steps.content: The passage opens with a saying that Dr. Malsufrido “carries more family secrets under his hat than any archbishop,” then reinforces this with the image that these secrets “remain nicely bottled up” inside him. This establishes that people confide in him and expect him to keep their secrets safe.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice B directly captures this trust: few feel concerned that he will divulge their confidences. A mistakenly focuses on appearance, C invents a claim about his own family discussions, and D credits professional expertise. That matches choice B — B is correct.
